questions.prompt: A water tank contains 500 liters at 2:00 PM. At 5:00 PM, it contains 350 liters. What is the rate of change of water volume, and what does it represent?
options: A) 50 liters per hour; the tank is losing water
B) -50 liters per hour; the tank is losing water at a constant rate
C) 150 liters per hour; the tank is filling up
D) -150 liters per hour; the tank is losing water
questions.correctAnswer: B) -50 liters per hour; the tank is losing water at a constant rate
questions.explanation: The slope (rate of change) is calculated as: (change in volume)/(change in time) = (350 - 500)/(5 - 2) = -150/3 = -50 liters per hour. The negative slope indicates the water volume is decreasing. The rate of change tells us the tank loses exactly 50 liters each hour, which is a constant rate of change. This interpretation is crucial for understanding real-world applications of slope.
